title: Building AI Agents & Workflows
subtitle: Most AI courses teach you to build demos. This one teaches you to build systems that stay running.
heroHeading: Three working AI systems in your business — by the end of the course
heroSubhead: Sixteen short written lessons. Three real systems you build and keep running. Workflows vs agents, tool choice, guardrails, debugging, and the maintenance reality most courses skip. No code. No hype.
whoItsFor: Business owners and operations leads ready to go beyond one-off prompts
Consultants and professionals who already automate with Zapier, Make, or n8n
Anyone who's finished AI Fundamentals and Claude Code and wants the next level
Builders who want real systems in production, not more demos
whatYoullLearn: The difference between workflows and agents — and when to use each
The minimum viable stack for non-coders (n8n + Claude + your business tools)
How to build three real systems: email triage, research agent, meeting-to-task automation
Error handling, logging, guardrails, and kill switches that make systems trustworthy
The maintenance reality: keeping systems running over time

modules.title: Workflows vs. agents — what you're actually building
modules.description: Get the foundations right before you build. The difference between workflows and agents, what actually delivers ROI, the minimum viable stack, and n8n running on your machine in 15 minutes.

lessons.title: Workflows vs. agents — know the difference
What actually delivers ROI (and what doesn't)
Your stack: n8n, Claude, and the minimum viable toolkit

modules.title: Build your first workflow
modules.description: Build your first real workflow end to end. The anatomy of triggers, nodes, and actions, an email-triage system, debugging when it breaks, and the error handling and logging that make it bulletproof.

lessons.title: Anatomy of a workflow: triggers, nodes, actions
Making it bulletproof: error handling and logging

modules.title: Build your first agent
modules.description: Step up to agents that decide their next move. What makes an agent different from a workflow, an end-to-end research agent, giving it memory and context, and the guardrails that keep it in its lane.

lessons.title: Guardrails — keeping agents in their lane

modules.title: Beyond building: connecting, scaling, knowing when to stop
modules.description: Beyond the build. Connecting AI to your real business tools, when multi-agent systems are worth it, the maintenance reality most courses skip, and a 30/60/90 portfolio plan.

lessons.title: Build #3: Connecting AI to your real business tools
Multi-agent systems: when they make sense (and when they don't)
Running AI systems over time: the maintenance reality

faqs.question: Who is this course for?
faqs.answer: Business owners and operations leads ready to go beyond one-off prompts. If you've already automated with Zapier, Make, or n8n — or finished AI Fundamentals and Claude Code Mastery — you're in the right place.

faqs.question: Do I need any prior experience?
faqs.answer: Yes. This is the advanced course. We assume you're comfortable with AI tools and have a working understanding of automation concepts. The AI Fundamentals and Claude Code Mastery courses cover the prerequisites.

faqs.question: How is the course delivered?
faqs.answer: Text-first. Each lesson is a short, well-written page with a small interactive exercise inside it and a quick check at the end. No long video runtimes, no playback speed gymnastics.

faqs.question: How long do I have access?
faqs.answer: Lifetime access. You buy once and keep it. Future updates to the course are included at no extra cost.

faqs.question: Is there a certificate?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass the final assessment with 70% or above and you'll get a verifiable certificate at a shareable URL (/certificate/[id]). The link works for anyone — no AICraft account required to verify it.

faqs.question: Can I get a refund?
faqs.answer: Yes. If the course isn't a fit within 14 days of purchase, email us and we'll refund you in full. No long forms.

faqs.question: Will I actually use this in my work?
faqs.answer: By design — you build three real systems during the course: an email triage workflow, a research agent, and a meeting-to-task automation. Not demos. Systems that stay running.
